metrics.title: Spot ETF AUM
metrics.label: US Spot Bitcoin ETFs — $42B+ Cumulative Inflows

metrics.title: Exchange Reserves
metrics.label: BTC Leaving Exchanges — Whale Accumulation Signal

moat.description: Absolute scarcity and the largest decentralized network effect in history.
moat.analysisSummary: Bitcoin's moat is built on **Math and Decentralization**:

analysisPoints.title: Absolute Scarcity
analysisPoints.text: Only 21 million will ever exist. Unlike fiat or even gold, the supply curve is perfectly inelastic to demand.

analysisPoints.title: Network Effect
analysisPoints.text: As the first and largest crypto asset, Bitcoin has the most liquidity, securest chain, and widest institutional support.

analysisPoints.title: Property Rights
analysisPoints.text: A global, permissionless system for storing value that is independent of any central bank or government.

growth.description: Institutional adoption via spot ETFs ($95.9B AUM, $42B+ cumulative inflows) and sovereign treasury integration (US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve formalized). Bitcoin hit a new all-time high of $126,080 in January 2026 — validating the post-halving cycle thesis — before pulling back to $66,194 on geopolitical risk-off. Cycle peak window (April–October 2026) remains ahead.

valuation.description: At ~$66,194 — down 47% from the January 2026 all-time high of $126,080 — BTC is in a post-ATH consolidation with geopolitical risk (Middle East) and ETF outflow weeks driving the pullback. Spot ETF AUM remains $95.9B with $42B+ cumulative inflows. Historically, cycle peaks arrive 12–18 months post-halving (April–October 2026 window). Trading between bear ($50,000) and base ($160,000).

bear.description: Extended geopolitical crisis combined with US recession triggers sustained ETF outflows and a full post-halving cycle drawdown to prior ATH support.
points: Middle East escalation causes sustained risk-off across all asset classes; BTC ETF weekly outflows exceed $500M for 6+ consecutive weeks
US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve execution is delayed by Congressional opposition, removing a key sovereign demand catalyst
On-chain activity continues declining (active addresses at multi-year lows) as retail exits; miner capitulation accelerates at sub-$55,000

base.description: Post-halving cycle peak arrives in mid-2026 as ETF inflows re-accelerate and the US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve formalizes, driving BTC to a new ATH above the January 2026 high.
points: Spot ETF AUM surpasses $200B as the halving-driven supply squeeze combines with steady institutional inflows from pension funds and endowments adding 1–2% BTC allocations
US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve begins active accumulation — confirmed purchases removing supply overhang and signaling sovereign legitimacy that triggers BRICS+ central bank interest
Morgan Stanley's low-fee spot BTC ETF launches, unlocking Morgan Stanley's $3.5T wealth management client base and accelerating retail re-entry

bull.description: Sovereign wealth fund adoption and a G7 currency crisis trigger a generational flight to Bitcoin as the world's reserve-quality decentralized hard asset.
points: Multiple G20 sovereign wealth funds formally add BTC to reserve portfolios — following the US Strategic Reserve precedent — creating a nation-state accumulation cycle with no precedent in financial history
US dollar loses reserve currency status at the margin as BRICS+ gold/BTC settlement framework captures 25%+ of global trade volume, triggering USD diversification at scale
Corporate treasury adoption surpasses 1,000 companies globally following Strategy Inc.'s playbook; combined corporate holdings exceed 3% of circulating supply

learnedInterfaces.note: Not applicable — Bitcoin is a decentralized monetary protocol, not a software interface product.

businessLogic.note: Not applicable — Bitcoin's protocol rules are immutable by design; there is no configurable business logic layer. This is a feature, not a vulnerability.

publicDataAccess.note: Not applicable — the Bitcoin blockchain is fully public and transparent by design; data access is not a competitive moat for a monetary protocol.

talentScarcity.note: Bitcoin core development talent remains specialized, but the protocol is intentionally simple and resistant to complex change.

bundling.note: Not applicable — Bitcoin is intentionally single-purpose; bundling products or features would undermine its core simplicity and security guarantees.

proprietaryData.note: The Bitcoin blockchain is an immutable, cryptographically secured, tamper-proof record of all value transfers since 2009.

regulatoryLockIn.note: SEC ETF approvals, FASB accounting treatment, sovereign wealth fund adoption, and growing legal tender status in multiple nations.

networkEffects.note: The largest crypto network with 52.4M+ unique holders and the broadest institutional support — Metcalfe's Law compounding since 2009.

transactionEmbedding.note: Bitcoin is primarily a store of value rather than a daily transaction medium; Layer 2 Lightning is growing but not yet embedded.

systemOfRecord.note: The original, most battle-tested, and most secure decentralized system of record — 15 years with zero successful protocol attacks.

tenMoats.verdict: Bitcoin's mathematical scarcity and decentralized consensus make it uniquely immune to AI disruption. No AI can inflate the supply, hack the network, or replicate the 15-year institutional trust-building.
